_doc: Hand-built faithful-replay fixtures for a UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities AppRequestTrigger TimeoutException (SignalR connection did not establish). Signatures and stack frames are source-grounded (HubConnectionService.EnsureChannelConnectedAsync 60s timeout; AppRequestTrigger.GetWorkflowRequest -> bookmark Throw). Substring dispatch, first match wins.

rules._doc: Error-level log row repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
The Error-level set answers any logs variant.
Pending -> Running -> Faulted; the ~66s Running-to-Faulted gap reflects the 60s SignalR establish wait.

Two spans: RobotJob (TimeoutException stack) and the Apps Request Trigger span (FullTypeName UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.AppRequestTrigger, ConnectionMode=SignalR). No invoke-workflow child span — the trigger never received a request because the channel never established.
Alternate trace-retrieval form; same span payload.
Positional TraceId form; same span payload.
Substring dispatch routes --state Faulted variants here too; the failing job is the most recent row.
docsai queries always passthrough — query strings vary between runs.

unmocked_default._doc: Permissive fallback: any unmatched uip command returns an empty array.

expected_calls.description: Triage must resolve the Shared folder key before listing jobs.
Agent must list jobs in folder Shared to identify the most recent (faulted) job.
Agent must fetch the failing job to read the System.TimeoutException from UiPath.WorkflowEvents.Activities.AppRequestTrigger (SignalR connection did not establish within 60 seconds).

Cause disambiguation: the Apps Request Trigger span shows ConnectionMode=SignalR and the absence of any invoke-workflow child span, pinning the fault to a failed channel establishment rather than an in-workflow error or a hub-bootstrap failure.
OPTIONAL — Error-level log repeats the exception already visible in jobs get.
OPTIONAL — state transitions show the ~60s establish wait before the fault.
